subject: Human Resources
title: World War II and Women in the Workforce
description: Learn World War II and Women in the Workforce in one minute.

hook.text: In 1942, millions of American men went to war. Factories needed workers. Women answered the call.

buildup.text: Rosie the Riveter became the symbol of 6 million women who entered manufacturing, welding, and engineering.

discovery.text: Women proved they could do every job men did. Companies had to redesign recruiting, training, and facilities.

twist.text: After the war, most women were pushed out of their jobs. But the precedent was set—permanently.

climax.text: WWII shattered the myth that women couldn't do industrial work and planted seeds for workplace equality.

punchline.text: Rosie proved capability has no gender.

quiz.question: How did WWII change the workforce?
options: Millions of women entered industrial jobs for the first time
